rationale: The citing paper uses Zauberman et al. (2009) as a core theoretical basis for its mechanism linking subjective time perception to intertemporal discounting. In the theory section, the authors explicitly rely on that paper to argue that greater attention to delay can lengthen perceived duration and increase present bias, and they build hypotheses from this logic. They also position their contribution as adding a new external cue (spatial orientation of options) to this established subjective-time account, rather than just mentioning the work in background lists.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Zauberman et al. (2009) by introducing spatial display orientation as an antecedent that shifts attention to delay and anticipated duration, which then affects intertemporal choice. It preserves the cited paper's subjective-time-to-discounting logic while adding a new contextual driver.
